blocks.subhead: Turn 10 engineer racing games to a standard that borders on the obsessive, and their studio site deserved the same care. This is the team behind Forza, shown as themselves.
blocks.meta: Studio site & identity · Xbox Game Studios
headline: Speed.
Precision.
Obsession.

blocks.label: The brief
heading: Built on the
studio’s DNA.
paragraphs: We started with a proper breakdown of the studio’s DNA. What drives them, what makes them distinct, and what sits behind all that machinery. The sketching kept returning to the same theme, the human touch behind their high-performance technology.

blocks.text: The visual system took its cues from motorsport design, race telemetry and the clean, no-nonsense look of technical schematics, grounded by a forward-leaning italic that keeps everything in motion.

blocks.caption: An interactive representation of the studio office.

blocks.label: The idea
heading: The office,
made interactive.
paragraphs: Early on we settled on representing the studio in an interactive, dynamic way, an online version of their office that shows each discipline in its natural habitat. Initial sketches were made in Photoshop, then laid out for our 3D modeller.
We tested building the model in WebGL, but the fidelity expected of a leader in 3D graphics meant outputting the animations as video, a library of looped sequences triggered by interaction.
Click an area of the office and you’re given a flythrough and an animated lobby for each discipline. Every one needed its own content, right down to the sticky notes on the whiteboards.

blocks.label: The result
heading: Absolute focus,
absolute control.
paragraphs: From the isometric studio map to the high-impact timeline, every element was tuned to echo Turn 10’s philosophy of absolute focus and absolute control. The result is a studio presence that moves as quickly as the games it makes.

blocks.quote: Michael is one of my very favourite people I have ever worked with. He is talented, kind, and puts his all into everything he focuses on. His enthusiasm for the new, to innovate, and his ability to take feedback on board in a way that just continually lifts his work up and up is really quite rare. I am absolutely convinced that he would make a fantastic addition to any design team.
blocks.name: Jonathan Hill

items.value: Brand identity, interactive direction, UX, content
